what_it_does: A local HTTP service that owns and manages held-open Claude Code subprocesses, exposing spawn/resume/fork/inject/interrupt/teardown via HTTP and a mailbox of deliver/wake/consult intents, while enforcing concurrency, permission, and per-turn wall-clock policies and emitting durable agent_sessions events.
observations: Binds 127.0.0.1 only and deliberately has no env var to widen exposure.
Reads COMPANY_FABRIC_CONCURRENCY, COMPANY_FABRIC_PERMISSION, and COMPANY_FABRIC_TURN_TIMEOUT_S at call time with defaults 3, plan, and 900 seconds.
Uses held-open stdin with --input-format stream-json and --output-format stream-json for turn injection.
Maintains a per-session state machine: starting -> idle <-> busy -> closed.
Consumes mailbox intents from agent_sessions/mail.jsonl using a byte-offset cursor ref and holds the cursor when a target is busy.
Is the sole writer of agent_sessions.* events onto the shared events.jsonl.
Provides a consent-gated R1-prime bridge-session profile with a wider --allowedTools set including Bash/git/LSP/web plus mcp__company.
Derives spawn-flag posture (locked/consent/safe/hazard) from the Mirror-Registry rules rather than storing posture locally.
Supports an ollama provider that runs `ollama launch claude --model <tag> -- <claude args>` instead of using ANTHROPIC_* env.
Drains each childs stderr in a dedicated thread to avoid pipe-buffer blocking.
Declares every Claude stdout event via render_declaration and records undeclared/family-fallback drops.
Extracts cost and token usage from result events for durable turn records.
Implements point-in-time session launches via runtime/session_pointintime for at=... wake/consult intents.
Adds /channel-reply and /channel-send POST endpoints not listed in the SUPERVISOR_ROUTES contract tuple.
standouts: Extensive inline law/audit commentary framing each design choice as a recorded ruling.
Registry-is-truth posture derivation for spawn flags with a local assembly table for emission shape only.
Consent-not-lockdown security model: the wider bridge-session surface is always available but requires an explicit operator_consent signal.
Ollama launcher command construction and PATH fix for the systemd environment.
stderr drain thread as the fix for resume spawns exiting with rc=1 due to a full pipe buffer.
Point-in-time session materialization integrated into mailbox intent handling.
conventions: Call-time environment reads for live configuration changes without restart.
TeachingRefusal exceptions carry explicit guidance about the limit and next steps.
Durable event writes raise on failure; non-durable writes log and continue.
Daemon threads for reader, stderr drain, watchdog, and mailbox loops.
Pure command-builder static methods that are unit-testable without spawning a real Claude process.
Type hints and sys.path insertion to import sibling modules.
Global SUP singleton accessed by the HTTP handler class.
concerns: Global mutable SUP is referenced by H without a None guard, so importing the module outside main could raise AttributeError on HTTP requests.
/channel-reply and /channel-send are handled in do_POST but absent from SUPERVISOR_ROUTES, creating a contract-drift hazard with the route crosscheck.
spawn_bridge_session duplicates the Popen, thread startup, wait_init, and emit logic from spawn rather than delegating to a common helper, increasing divergence risk.
Internal server errors in do_POST are returned to the client but not logged server-side, making debugging harder.
_apply_spawn_flags mutates the command list in place for swap kinds, which is acceptable but side-effectful.
The concurrency cap is checked under lock but the subsequent Popen creation happens outside it, leaving a small race window between cap check and registration.
main uses os._exit in the signal handler after teardown, which abruptly terminates the process.
notes: This file is the fabric's session spine: a heavily documented, audit-citation-rich service that tries to make every security boundary explicit and every refusal teachable.
questions: How exactly does introspection.rules.classify map a flag name to a posture using the claude-code signal sets?
What is the full contract and behavior of runtime.cc_channels for /channel-reply and /channel-send?
How does runtime.session_pointintime.materialize_at_point handle transcript prefixes and where does it write the materialized copy?
What does render_declaration do when it encounters an unknown event family, and how is the registry gap surfaced?
Has the interrupt control_request JSON shape been verified against a real Claude Code stream-json process?
How is recovery handled if a durable event write fails during normal operation?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is the single-owner session supervisor with HTTP and mailbox interfaces. The code adds point-in-time at-launch and channel endpoints that extend but do not contradict the documented scope.
apparent_themes: Single writer / single owner for live sessions and events
No orphans and no silent drops
Held-open stream transport for live sessions
intention_signals: The file is trying to be a trustworthy, operator-auditable runtime spine for the fabric: it wants to keep Claude Code sessions alive, route work to them safely, and only open a wider tool surface when the operator explicitly consents, while recording every significant lifecycle and security decision.
novelty: The combination of registry-derived spawn-flag posture, a named consent-gated bridge-session profile, ollama-launcher command integration, point-in-time session materialization, and pervasive inline law/audit citations makes this more than a typical process supervisor.
contribution: It contributes the live Claude Code process ownership layer, the HTTP control surface, the mailbox intent consumer, the durable agent_sessions event stream, the concurrency/timeout/permission enforcement, and the consent-gated wider-tool spawn profile that a fused system would need to keep.
summary_for_embedding: A local HTTP service that supervises held-open Claude Code subprocesses, routes deliver/wake/consult mailbox intents, enforces concurrency and turn timeouts, and supports a consent-gated wider-tool bridge-session spawn profile.
intention_for_embedding: To be the single owner of live Claude Code sessions for the fabric, exposing spawn/inject/interrupt/teardown over HTTP and mailbox while keeping dangerous capability widening behind an explicit operator-consent beat.

symbols.does: Reads the live concurrency cap from COMPANY_FABRIC_CONCURRENCY, defaulting to 3.
symbols.description: Returns the maximum number of live supervised sessions and consult fan copies allowed at call time. The default follows the implement.py CONCURRENCY_CAP precedent and is intentionally read at call time so tests can monkeypatch it.

symbols.does: Reads the live permission posture from COMPANY_FABRIC_PERMISSION, defaulting to plan.
symbols.description: Returns the permission mode for supervised sessions at call time. plan means read-only; acceptEdits or bypassPermissions must be opted in explicitly.

symbols.does: Reads the per-turn wall-clock timeout from COMPANY_FABRIC_TURN_TIMEOUT_S, defaulting to 900 seconds.
symbols.description: Returns the enforced ceiling for a single turn in seconds. The default follows implement.py DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_S and is read at call time to support short acceptance-test timeouts.

symbols.does: Returns the current UTC time as an ISO 8601 string.
symbols.description: Utility used to timestamp events and session records consistently.

symbols.does: Exception subclass for refusal responses that include teaching guidance.
symbols.description: Raised when the supervisor refuses an action because of a policy boundary. The message names the limit, the live state, and the way forward, and HTTP handlers map it to 403, 429, or 409.

symbols.does: Loads and caches the claude-code PlatformEntry signal_sets from the platform registry.
symbols.description: Provides the registry-derived rule inputs used to classify spawn-flag posture. Fails loudly if the claude-code entry is missing, because the hand-maintained posture table was deleted and the registry is now the sole truth.

symbols.does: Derives a spawn flag's posture from the Mirror-Registry rules.
symbols.description: Calls introspection.rules.classify over the cached claude-code signal sets and returns locked, hazard, consent, safe, or unmatched. Locked transport-invariant and hazard flags are refused; consent flags require the operator-consent beat.

symbols.does: Validates and appends registry-declared spawn flags onto a command list.
symbols.description: Looks up each flag key in SPAWN_FLAG_ASSEMBLY, derives its posture from the registry, refuses locked/hazard flags and consent flags without consent, and emits the flag using bool/value/csv/repeat/swap assembly kinds. Swap rows replace an existing flag value in place.

symbols.does: Represents one owned Claude Code subprocess and its supervisor-side state.
symbols.description: Holds the session id, claude session id, cwd, resume/fork metadata, state machine, I/O locks, event deque, subscriber queues, and turn tracking. The state machine is starting -> idle <-> busy -> closed.

symbols.does: Initializes a new supervised session record.
symbols.description: Assigns a local handle, stores spawn parameters, sets state to starting, initializes activity and turn timers, creates the stdin lock and event/subscriber structures, and leaves the subprocess reference empty until spawn assigns it.

symbols.does: Returns a JSON-serializable snapshot of the session.
symbols.description: Includes id, claude_session_id, name, cwd, state, resume/fork flags, creation time, last activity, turn count, pid, close reason, and profile. Used by GET /sessions and HTTP responses.

symbols.does: Checks whether a key matches the session's local or Claude session id.
symbols.description: Used by find to resolve session://<id> targets and HTTP session parameters.

symbols.does: Pulls cost and token usage from a Claude result event.
symbols.description: Reads usage, modelUsage/model_usage, total_cost_usd/cost_usd, and model fields, returning a flat dict only when at least one value is present. Captures usage that the supervisor previously discarded.

symbols.does: Owns the fleet of supervised sessions and all supervisor loops.
symbols.description: Coordinates session lifecycle, event fanout, durable event emission, mailbox consumption, HTTP backend state, and the watchdog. All fleet mutations happen under self.lock.

symbols.does: Initializes the supervisor with a store and empty fleet.
symbols.description: Stores the FsStore, creates the sessions dict and lock, computes the mailbox leaf path, and creates the stop event used by daemon loops.

symbols.does: Appends an event to the shared events.jsonl via the store.
symbols.description: Durable writes are claims whose loss changes behavior and are re-raised on failure; non-durable writes are narration and log-and-continue. This makes event-write failures visible without killing the watchdog.

symbols.does: Sequences and broadcasts an event to a session's subscribers.
symbols.description: Assigns a per-session sequence number and timestamp, appends the event to the session's bounded deque, and pushes it to every subscriber queue. Silently drops subscribers that fail.

symbols.does: Returns all sessions whose state is not closed.
symbols.description: Used by the concurrency cap check and teardown_all to count currently owned sessions.

symbols.does: Raises a teaching refusal if adding sessions would exceed the cap.
symbols.description: Compares the requested addition against fabric_concurrency and the current live count. The refusal message names the cap, the env var, live sessions, and how to free a slot.

symbols.does: Purely constructs the base claude command for a supervised session.
symbols.description: Builds the held-open transport head with -p, --input-format stream-json, --output-format stream-json, --verbose, permission mode, strict company MCP config, and mcp__company-only allowlist. Optionally adds model, effort, fallback, settings, add-dir, include-partial-messages, debug, safe-mode, bare, and an ollama launcher prefix.

symbols.does: Returns an empty dict for provider environment overrides.
symbols.description: Documents that the ollama provider routes via the launcher command rather than ANTHROPIC_* env injection. Kept as a stub so future env-only providers have a home without changing call sites.

symbols.does: Resolves bridge-session capabilities into a concrete --allowedTools list.
symbols.description: Maps git, lsp, web, and edit capabilities to their Atlas-grounded tool names and refuses computer and browser with teaching reasons. Also validates explicit extra_tools so the host/rail boundary cannot slip in as a raw specifier.

symbols.does: Purely constructs the wider-allowlist bridge-session command.
symbols.description: Delegates to _build_spawn_cmd with the bridge-session permission posture, then swaps the --allowedTools value to the wider comma-joined profile set. Raises TeachingRefusal for unavailable capabilities.

symbols.does: Spawns a new supervised Claude Code session.
symbols.description: Validates registry flags, checks the concurrency cap, creates a Supervised record, resolves the binary, builds the command, applies registry flags, sets up child env and PATH for ollama, starts Popen with reader and stderr-drain threads, emits a durable spawned event, and optionally settles to idle and injects a prompt.

symbols.does: Spawns a consent-gated wider-tool bridge-session.
symbols.description: Requires operator_consent=True, resolves the wider toolset before registering the session, starts the process, and emits a durable spawned event recording the profile, consent, and allowlist. Optionally injects an initial prompt.

symbols.does: Reads a child process's stderr for its entire lifetime.
symbols.description: Prevents the OS pipe buffer from filling and blocking the child. Retains only the last 50 lines for close diagnostics.

symbols.does: Declares a raw Claude event and fans the declared form.
symbols.description: Uses render_declaration to produce a declared event, fans it alongside the legacy event, and emits a render_drop durable event for undeclared or family-fallback emits. Catches and logs declaration failures so the reader thread survives.

symbols.does: Parses a session's stdout stream for the life of the process.
symbols.description: Handles system/init to capture claude_session_id and cwd, assistant text and tool_use blocks, result events to complete turns, and EOF/process exit to close the session. Non-JSON lines are ignored.

symbols.does: Finalizes a completed turn and emits durable records.
symbols.description: Computes duration, updates turn count and state to idle, fans a done event, emits a durable agent_sessions.turn event with extracted usage, replies to the originating mailbox intent, and emits an idle event.

symbols.does: Looks up a session by local id or claude session id.
symbols.description: Searches under self.lock and returns the matching Supervised instance or None.

symbols.does: Injects a user turn into a live session's stdin.
symbols.description: Refuses closed or busy sessions, writes a stream-json user message, transitions state to busy, records turn metadata, and fans an injected event. A failed stdin write closes the session.

symbols.does: Sends a control_request interrupt to a busy session's stdin.
symbols.description: Writes a stream-json control_request with subtype interrupt. Refuses if the session is not busy. The shape is documented as built-untested against a real Claude process.

symbols.does: Marks a session closed and optionally kills its process.
symbols.description: Sets state and close_reason, closes stdin, terminates and waits up to five seconds, kills if necessary, fans a closed event, and emits a durable agent_sessions.closed event.

symbols.does: Publicly closes and kills a single session.
symbols.description: No-op if already closed; otherwise calls _close with kill=True and reason teardown.

symbols.does: Tears down every live session.
symbols.description: Iterates the current live list under the lock and calls teardown on each, logging individual failures. Used by signal handlers and atexit.

symbols.does: Daemon loop that reaps sessions exceeding wall-clock limits.
symbols.description: Checks every WATCHDOG_POLL_S whether a busy turn has exceeded turn_timeout_s or a starting session has exceeded the same limit without init. Kills offending sessions and logs event-write failures without stopping the loop.

symbols.does: Reads the supervisor's mailbox cursor offset.
symbols.description: Returns the value stored in the cursor ref, or the current size of mail.jsonl on first boot so the supervisor never replays historical intents from before it started.

symbols.does: Returns the aggregation thread for a mailbox reply.
symbols.description: Uses the intent's thread field when present, falling back to the intent id, so replies from consult fans join the same conversation.

symbols.does: Appends a durable reply to the mailbox after a turn completes.
symbols.description: Stores the result text in the content-addressed store and appends a reply or error mail record via append_agent_mail with the intent's thread and re reference.

symbols.does: Appends a durable error reply for a refused or failed intent.
symbols.description: Logs the refusal to stderr, stores the reason text, and appends an error mail record to the intent's sender with thread and re.

symbols.does: Resolves the body text of a mailbox intent.
symbols.description: Prefers the content addressed by cas, falling back to an inline message field. Raises ValueError if neither is present.

symbols.does: Daemon loop that consumes mailbox intents.
symbols.description: Repeatedly calls _mail_pass from the current cursor and sleeps MAIL_POLL_S. Logs pass failures and continues.

symbols.does: Reads new mail lines and handles deliver/wake/consult intents.
symbols.description: Seeks from the cursor, parses complete lines, skips replies and foreign verbs, handles intents in order, advances the cursor and cursor ref on success, and holds the cursor when a deliver target is busy.

symbols.does: Validates the cwd for resuming a target session.
symbols.description: When a registry record with jsonl_path exists, uses resume_cwd_for to validate candidate directories by re-encoding the project dir; falls back to _cwd_for if validation fails or no record exists.

symbols.does: Resolves the resume directory for a target session.
symbols.description: Checks the intent's cwd hint first, then the newest agent_sessions.registered event for the claude_session_id, then a live session's cwd, returning None if none are found.

symbols.does: Routes a single mailbox intent to the appropriate action.
symbols.description: Handles deliver by injecting into a live session, wake by resuming and injecting, consult by forking N copies, and at=... by point-in-time materialization. Returns False only when a busy target requires head-of-line holding; all other outcomes are terminal replies.

symbols.does: Materializes and launches a session at a point in time.
symbols.description: For wake/consult intents carrying at=, uses session_pointintime to create a transcript copy, registers the new session, validates the resume cwd, spawns the copy, and injects the message. Supports N independent copies for consult.

symbols.does: HTTP request handler for the supervisor API.
symbols.description: Subclass of BaseHTTPRequestHandler implementing GET /health, /sessions, /watch and POST /spawn, /bridge-session, /channel-reply, /channel-send, /inject, /interrupt, /teardown. Maps TeachingRefusal to 403/429/409 and other exceptions to 500.

symbols.does: Suppresses default HTTP access logging.
symbols.description: Overrides BaseHTTPRequestHandler.log_message to a no-op so journald does not receive per-request access lines.

symbols.does: Sends a JSON HTTP response with explicit Content-Length.
symbols.description: Serializes the object, sets the response code and application/json content type, writes the length header, and writes the body.

symbols.does: Dispatches GET requests to health, sessions, or watch handlers.
symbols.description: Returns JSON for /health and /sessions, delegates /watch to the ndjson streamer, and returns a 404 teaching response for unknown paths.

symbols.does: Streams replay and live events for a session as ndjson.
symbols.description: Finds the session, appends a subscriber queue, replays buffered events, then blocks on the queue sending new events with periodic keepalives until the session closes or the client disconnects.

symbols.does: Dispatches POST requests to spawn, bridge-session, channel, inject, interrupt, and teardown handlers.
symbols.description: Parses JSON body, calls the corresponding SessionSupervisor method, and returns the session record or operation result. Catches TeachingRefusal to return 403/429/409 and other exceptions as 500.

symbols.does: Entry point that starts the supervisor service.
symbols.description: Parses the port, creates the FsStore and SessionSupervisor, starts watchdog and mailbox daemon threads, registers SIGTERM/SIGINT and atexit teardown, prints the bind line, and runs the ThreadingHTTPServer.

symbols.does: Signal handler that stops loops and tears down all sessions.
symbols.description: Sets the stop event, calls teardown_all, and exits the process with os._exit.
